metadata.episode: EP015 -- Sonnet 5 reprice: does cheaper cloud change the routing?
metadata.provenance: MEASURED: local from ep004_quality.json (run_quality.py); cloud per-bucket supplied. Routing is deterministic arithmetic over those measured splits.
metadata.exam: same 15-task graded exam as EP004/EP006 (temp 0, deterministic graders)
metadata.policy: escalate ['math'] to cloud; rest stays local. Policy fixed in advance from EP004's measured weak bucket -- NOT by peeking at grades.
metadata.honesty: Routing is only as good as your benchmark of where the small model breaks. Without that map you overpay (escalate all) or eat errors (escalate none).

local.label: All-local (3B) accuracy
local.context: free on an 8GB laptop, but weak on its weak bucket

routing.label: Smart routing accuracy
routing.context: escalates only 27% of tasks to the cloud

cloud.label: All-cloud accuracy
cloud.context: top score, but the frontier price on every call (~97x)

savings.label: Cloud bill cut by routing
savings.context: same accuracy as all-cloud, a fraction of the cloud calls
